reasoning_trace.signal: 2 full-price products at ₹1,450-2,200, strong ratings, editorial placement
reasoning_trace.proves: Premium demand exists at full price; trickle-down potential to value segment
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether this demand translates to mass-market at ₹699-899
reasoning_trace.tension: Contrasts with Internal POS failure at similar price; suggests premium-only viability

reasoning_trace.signal: 2 products at ₹899-949 with 15-20% discount, rank 3 and 8, high reviews
reasoning_trace.proves: Strong organic demand at mass retail with strategic pricing
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is style-driven or price-driven (discount is low, so likely style-driven)
reasoning_trace.tension: Aligns with Nykaa on demand existence; conflicts with Internal POS underperformance

reasoning_trace.signal: 1 imitation product at ₹349, 1,800 units sold, 45 resellers, 3.4 rating
reasoning_trace.proves: Emerging volume signal in tier-2/3/4 at lower price point
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether this is genuine Chanderi demand or just cheap imitation demand
reasoning_trace.tension: Weak presence vs strong Nykaa/Myntra; price gap suggests different customer base

reasoning_trace.signal: Similar SKU underperformed: 38% sell-through, 18% return rate, metro-only success
reasoning_trace.proves: Chanderi silk has narrow appeal in our store base; durability concerns
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether a lower price or blended fabric would perform better
reasoning_trace.tension: HIGH conflict with Nykaa/Myntra strong signals; suggests our customer differs

reasoning_trace.proves: Competitors have conviction in Chanderi straight kurti at ₹899
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether ads are driving demand or capturing existing demand
reasoning_trace.tension: Supports Myntra/Ajio demand; conflicts with Internal POS failure

reasoning_trace.signal: 10 haul videos, 10 creators, 0.1 affiliate links/video
reasoning_trace.proves: Low commercial push; organic creator interest exists but not strong
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether this is a genuine consumer trend or just niche interest
reasoning_trace.tension: Low buzz vs strong marketplace signals; suggests trend is not viral

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Internal POS contradicts external demand signals (Nykaa, Myntra, Meta Ads). Meesho weak vs Nykaa strong. YouTube low buzz vs marketplace high rank.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: Nykaa, Myntra, and Meta Ads all show strong demand for Chanderi straight kurti at ₹899-1,450 with low discount. Premium and mass retail align.

chanderi-straight.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: External demand strong (Nykaa, Myntra, Meta Ads) but Internal POS shows prior failure with similar product

conflicts.myntra_says: Strong organic demand at strategic pricing

conflicts.buyer_question: Is our customer base different from Nykaa/Myntra shoppers for this style?
conflicts.proves: External demand exists for Chanderi straight kurti at ₹899-1,450
conflicts.cannot_prove: That our specific store base will replicate that demand

conflicts.title: Meesho weak vs Nykaa strong
conflicts.meesho_says: Weak presence: 1 imitation product at ₹349, 1,800 units

conflicts.proves: Premium and mass retail demand is strong

conflicts.title: YouTube low buzz vs marketplace high rank
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the demand driven by paid ads rather than organic buzz?

convergences.title: Nykaa, Myntra, and Meta Ads converge on strong demand for Chanderi straight kurti at ₹899-1,450
convergences.detail: Nykaa shows full-price premium demand, Myntra shows high rank with low discount, Meta Ads show competitor conviction. All point to a validated trend in premium and mass retail.
convergences.proves: The style has genuine demand at ₹899-1,450 with low discount dependency
convergences.cannot_prove: That this demand extends to our store base or to lower price points

chanderi-straight.upside_summary: External signals are strong: Nykaa, Myntra, and competitor ads all show robust demand for Chanderi straight kurti at ₹899-1,450 with minimal discounting. The trend is validated across premium and mass retail, suggesting a genuine style preference rather than price-driven demand.
chanderi-straight.catch_summary: Internal POS data directly contradicts: a similar Chanderi silk kurti underperformed with 38% sell-through and 18% returns, working only in metro stores. Our core customer found the fabric too delicate. Meesho presence is weak and YouTube buzz is low, indicating the trend may not have mass organic traction.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, full-price products, editorial placement, high ratings

myntra.reason: High rank, high reviews, low discount, no noise flags

meesho.reason: Weak presence, imitation product, low rating, but 1,800 units sold

internal.reason: Direct sales data with clear outcome and buyer note

chanderi-straight.bet_rationale: External demand is strong but internal POS contradicts. A small trial in metro stores (where prior SKU worked) can validate whether a lower price point or blended fabric changes the outcome.
chanderi-straight.key_uncertainty: Will a ₹699-899 Chanderi straight kurti with a blended fabric (e.g., Chanderi-cotton) perform better in our stores than the previous pure silk version?

Competitor ad duration extends beyond 28 days

Regional demand data for tier-2 cities beyond Meesho

reasoning_trace.signal: Absent: 0 products found. Block print too basic for premium positioning.

reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove demand is for block-print specifically (product names generic); no velocity data.

reasoning_trace.proves: Block print cotton kurtis are a proven winner for our store base; under-ordered last time.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove current designs will repeat success (design fatigue risk).

reasoning_trace.proves: Competitors see enough demand to invest in ads; Aurelia's long-running ad indicates conviction.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove ad-driven demand is organic; could be pushing slow-moving inventory.

reasoning_trace.proves: Not a creator-driven trend; organic consumer interest is low.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Cannot prove lack of consumer demand; block print may be too basic for haul content.
reasoning_trace.tension: Conflicts with strong marketplace and internal signals; suggests trend is not viral.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Myntra discount dependency vs Meesho/Internal clean demand; YouTube low buzz vs marketplace strength.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: Meesho, Internal POS, and Meta Ads all point to real demand for block-print cotton kurtis at value prices.

blockprint-cotton.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Myntra shows discount-driven demand, but Meesho and Internal POS confirm clean value demand. YouTube buzz is low, but Meta ads show competitor conviction.

conflicts.title: Myntra discount dependency vs Meesho/Internal clean demand
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong presence but 2/3 products have moderate discount with unclear driver
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong presence, mostly no discount, high ratings
conflicts.internal_says: Past success with similar product at full price, low markdowns
conflicts.buyer_question: Is Myntra's demand real or propped up by discounting?
conflicts.proves: Myntra demand may be price-sensitive; Meesho and Internal show genuine value demand.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether Myntra's discount is accelerating a real trend or creating artificial demand.

conflicts.title: YouTube low buzz vs marketplace and ad strength
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a genuine consumer trend or just paid push?
conflicts.proves: Marketplace and ads show commercial activity; YouTube shows low organic interest.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove whether low YouTube buzz means low consumer demand or just low content appeal.

convergences.title: Value-fashion demand for block-print cotton kurtis is real
convergences.detail: Meesho (10 products, high ratings), Internal POS (78% sell-through), and Meta Ads (2 competitors advertising) all point to genuine demand at value price points.
convergences.proves: The style has proven demand across price-sensitive and mass retail channels.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the exact design or color preferences; design fatigue risk exists.

blockprint-cotton.upside_summary: Block-print cotton A-line kurtis are a proven category for your store (78% sell-through, 44% margin). Meesho shows strong demand at low prices with high ratings. Competitors are advertising, indicating market confidence. Under-ordering last time suggests room to increase commitment.
blockprint-cotton.catch_summary: Myntra's demand is partly discount-driven, raising questions about price dependency. YouTube buzz is low, meaning this is not a viral trend. Design fatigue could reduce repeat performance. Nykaa's absence confirms this is value-fashion, not premium.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but discount distortion reduces signal clarity.

meesho.reason: Live scraped, high ratings, no discount distortion.

internal.reason: Ground truth from own stores; high sell-through and margin.

blockprint-cotton.bet_rationale: Internal POS confirms this is a proven winner with under-ordering last time. Meesho and competitor ads reinforce demand. Myntra's discount dependency is a medium conflict but does not outweigh the convergence of clean signals.
blockprint-cotton.key_uncertainty: Will current block-print designs match last season's performance, or is there design fatigue?

Meesho reseller count accelerates
YouTube haul videos increase >50% month-over-month
Competitor ad campaigns end or reduce spend

reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether a new colorway or modern design could unlock South/East demand

reasoning_trace.proves: Emerging competitor conviction; Libas is testing the style
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether ad will sustain beyond 7 days; only 1 competitor, not multiple

reasoning_trace.proves: Low commercial push; creators featuring organically, not paid
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether organic interest is genuine or just incidental; low volume
reasoning_trace.tension: Low buzz suggests trend is not yet mainstream; aligns with emerging signals elsewhere

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Nykaa premium vs. Meesho value pricing; Internal POS regional vs. any national assumption; Myntra clean but single product
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: All sources show positive but early signals; no source contradicts demand existence; regional pattern consistent across Meesho and Internal POS

bandhani-straight.headline: All sources converge on emerging demand for Bandhani Straight Kurti, but regional pattern from Internal POS limits national scope

convergences.title: All sources show positive demand signal
convergences.detail: Nykaa (emerging), Myntra (moderate clean), Meesho (emerging volume), Internal POS (regional success), Meta Ads (testing), YouTube (organic low buzz) all indicate demand exists.
convergences.proves: Bandhani Straight Kurti has genuine demand across price tiers and regions.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is strong enough for national rollout; regional limitations persist.

convergences.title: Regional pattern consistent across Meesho and Internal POS
convergences.detail: Meesho shows top regions Gujarat, Rajasthan, Maharashtra; Internal POS top regions Ahmedabad, Surat, Jaipur. Both point to Western India as core market.
convergences.proves: Bandhani demand is concentrated in Western India.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether other regions can be developed with different marketing or product variations.

bandhani-straight.upside_summary: Bandhani Straight Kurti shows clean demand signals across premium (Nykaa), mass (Myntra), and value (Meesho) segments. Meesho's accelerating reseller growth (+35% MoM) and Myntra's strategic pricing (20% off, 4.3★) indicate genuine style-driven demand, not just discount-driven. Internal POS confirms strong regional success in Western India with 52% sell-through.
bandhani-straight.catch_summary: The trend is regional, not national. Internal POS shows poor performance in South and East India. Nykaa presence is limited to one product at a higher price point. YouTube buzz is low, suggesting the trend hasn't gone mainstream yet. Only one competitor is advertising, indicating early stage.

nykaa.reason: Only 1 product found, no full-price signal, moderate rating.

myntra.reason: Clean signal: low discount, good rating, no noise flags. But only 1 product.

meesho.reason: Strong volume with accelerating reseller growth, good rating. Price-sensitive but genuine.

internal.reason: Real sales data with clear regional pattern. High relevance.

bandhani-straight.bet_rationale: All sources converge on positive demand, but the trend is regional and early-stage. A small trial in Western India (Gujarat, Rajasthan, Maharashtra) can validate the style at ₹499-799 before wider rollout.
bandhani-straight.key_uncertainty: Can a modern colorway or design variation unlock demand in South and East India?

At least 2 more competitors start advertising Bandhani kurtis on Meta
YouTube haul video count doubles in next 30 days

bandhani-straight.demand_integrity: Clean — no noise flags detected. Myntra product is Strategic Value Pricing. Meesho product has normal price sensitivity. Internal POS data is real sales.

reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether demand is style-driven or price-driven due to 35-40% discounts and paid visibility.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong signal but discount and paid ads raise quality concerns. Nykaa absent.

reasoning_trace.proves: Trend is available at value price points across tier-2/3/4 markets.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Actual demand velocity or reseller traction due to zero reviews and sales data.

reasoning_trace.proves: This format (kurti+palazzo set) has proven demand in our stores across metro cities.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether next season's designs will sustain same velocity or if design fatigue sets in.
reasoning_trace.tension: Strong internal success contradicts external saturation narrative. Supports deeper buy.

reasoning_trace.signal: 2 competitors advertising: Libas (35 days, strong conviction) and Westside (18 days, emerging).
reasoning_trace.proves: Competitors see enough demand to invest in long-running ads for similar sets.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether ad-driven demand is incremental or cannibalizing organic sales.
reasoning_trace.tension: Supports demand validation but aligns with Myntra's paid visibility flags.

reasoning_trace.signal: Low buzz: 10 haul videos from 9 creators, low affiliate density (0.3/video).
reasoning_trace.proves: Creators are featuring the trend organically, not commercially driven.
reasoning_trace.cannot_prove: Whether organic interest is translating to purchase intent or just content filler.
reasoning_trace.tension: Low buzz contrasts with strong marketplace presence. Suggests trend is not creator-led.

reasoning_trace.key_tensions: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Ajio strong; Myntra/Ajio discount+paid visibility vs internal POS clean success; Meesho presence without demand proof; YouTube low buzz vs competitor ad conviction.
reasoning_trace.key_convergences: Myntra/Ajio and Meesho both show strong presence; Internal POS confirms similar format success; Competitor ads validate demand.

fusion-palazzo.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Strong marketplace presence and internal success, but Nykaa absent and YouTube buzz low — discount-driven demand risk remains.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Ajio strong
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium D2C demand for this trend.
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong demand in mass retail with high review velocity.
conflicts.internal_says: Similar format was a top seller in our stores.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the trend truly broad-based or is it limited to discount-driven mass retail?
conflicts.proves: Trend is not validated at premium price points (₹800+).
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the trend will work at higher price points or if it's inherently value-fashion.

conflicts.title: Myntra/Ajio discount and paid visibility vs internal POS clean success
conflicts.myntra_says: High rank but 35-40% discount and paid visibility flags.
conflicts.meesho_says: Some deep discounts (85-93% off) suggest liquidation.
conflicts.internal_says: Past similar SKU sold well at full price with minimal markdown (12%).
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the current marketplace demand price-driven or style-driven?
conflicts.proves: Marketplace demand is partly inflated by discounts and ads.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the trend would sustain without discounts.

conflicts.title: YouTube low buzz vs competitor ad conviction
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the trend being pushed by paid ads or is there genuine organic interest?
conflicts.proves: Competitors are investing in ads, but organic creator buzz is low.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the low YouTube buzz indicates low consumer interest or just a non-creator-led trend.

convergences.title: Marketplace presence strong across Myntra/Ajio and Meesho
convergences.detail: Both mass retail and value platforms show multiple products for kurti+palazzo sets.
convergences.proves: The trend is widely available and stocked by multiple sellers.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the demand is genuine or driven by discounts/ads.

convergences.title: Internal POS confirms similar format success
convergences.detail: Past Printed Kurti + Palazzo Set achieved 82% sell-through, 38% margin, and stockouts.
convergences.proves: Our customer base has proven demand for this format at similar price points.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether next season's designs will repeat the same performance.

convergences.title: Competitor ads validate demand
convergences.detail: Libas and Westside running long-running ads for similar sets.
convergences.proves: Competitors see enough demand to invest in sustained ad campaigns.
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether the ads are driving incremental demand or just capturing existing search.

fusion-palazzo.upside_summary: Internal POS shows strong past success with a similar kurti+palazzo set, achieving 82% sell-through and stockouts in 68 stores. Competitors are investing in long-running ads, indicating sustained demand. The trend is widely available across mass retail and value platforms, suggesting broad market acceptance.
fusion-palazzo.catch_summary: Nykaa absence indicates no premium D2C demand, and Myntra/Ajio demand is partly discount-driven with paid visibility flags. YouTube buzz is low, suggesting the trend is not organically creator-led. Meesho products lack reviews and sales data, making demand quality uncertain.

nykaa.reason: No products found; absent presence provides no signal.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but discount and paid visibility flags reduce signal quality.

meesho.reason: Strong presence but zero reviews/sales data; deep discounts on some items.

internal.reason: High-quality past data with clear sell-through, margin, and repeat buy metrics.

fusion-palazzo.bet_rationale: Internal POS success and competitor ads support demand, but Nykaa absence and discount-driven marketplace signals create uncertainty. A small batch in representative stores can validate whether the trend holds without heavy discounting.
fusion-palazzo.key_uncertainty: Will the trend sustain at full price or near-full price in our stores, or is demand primarily price-driven?

eval-discount-trap.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa absent, Myntra shows subsidized liquidation, Meesho shows price-driven volume. No clean demand signal.

conflicts.title: Nykaa absent vs Myntra/Meesho strong
conflicts.nykaa_says: No premium demand; style not present in premium D2C.
conflicts.myntra_says: Strong presence but only via deep discount (65% off) with low rating.
conflicts.meesho_says: Strong volume at very low price (₹299).
conflicts.buyer_question: Is the demand real or purely price-driven?
conflicts.proves: The style has traction only at low price points or with heavy discount.
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the style has any intrinsic demand beyond price sensitivity.

conflicts.title: Myntra discount context vs Meesho volume
conflicts.myntra_says: Subsidized Liquidation: discount IS the demand.
conflicts.meesho_says: High volume but at ₹299; reseller growth not accelerating.
conflicts.buyer_question: Is Meesho volume also price-driven or is there genuine style preference?
conflicts.cannot_prove: Cannot prove style-driven demand at any price point.

convergences.title: Price-driven demand across Myntra and Meesho
convergences.detail: Both Myntra (65% off) and Meesho (₹299) indicate demand is heavily price-sensitive.
convergences.proves: The style appeals to bargain hunters, not full-price buyers.
convergences.cannot_prove: Cannot prove the style has any premium or style-driven demand.

eval-discount-trap.upside_summary: Meesho shows strong volume (8000+ units) at ₹299, indicating broad appeal in value-fashion. If the style can be sourced at a low cost, there may be a volume opportunity in tier-2/3 markets.
eval-discount-trap.catch_summary: Nykaa absence and Myntra's subsidized liquidation signal that demand is entirely price-driven. No premium or style-driven demand exists. Internal POS has no data, and no social or ad evidence supports organic interest.

nykaa.reason: No products found; premium D2C absent.

myntra.reason: Strong presence but heavily distorted by deep discount and low rating.

meesho.reason: High volume but at very low price; reseller growth not accelerating.

eval-discount-trap.bet_rationale: Evidence is insufficient and contradictory: Nykaa absent, Myntra shows price-driven demand only, Meesho volume is at rock-bottom price. No internal, social, or ad data to confirm genuine style demand. Monitor for clearer signals before committing capital.
eval-discount-trap.key_uncertainty: Is the Meesho volume driven by style preference or just extreme low price?
watch_triggers: Nykaa adds products for this style at full price
Meesho reseller growth accelerates
Competitor Meta ads appear for this style
YouTube haul videos emerge with organic buzz
eval-discount-trap.demand_integrity: Subsidized Liquidation on Myntra; price-driven on Meesho. No clean demand signal.

eval-premium-mirage.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Nykaa strong at full price, Meesho absent — premium-only trend, may not work for value-fashion

conflicts.title: Nykaa strong vs Meesho absent
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong premium demand at full price (₹1850 avg)
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a premium-only trend that will fail in value-fashion channels?
conflicts.proves: Premium demand exists at full price; mass-market demand is absent
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether the trend will expand to lower price points or remain niche

convergences.title: Nykaa and Myntra both show clean demand signals
convergences.detail: Nykaa has 3 full-price products with high ratings; Myntra has 1 product at low discount with good reviews. Both indicate genuine style preference, not discount-driven.
convergences.proves: The style has appeal at premium and mid-tier price points
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is broad enough for mass adoption

eval-premium-mirage.upside_summary: Nykaa shows strong full-price demand at ₹1850 with high ratings, indicating premium appeal. Myntra confirms moderate demand at strategic pricing. The style has clean signals at higher price tiers.
eval-premium-mirage.catch_summary: Meesho absence is a major red flag — no demand in price-sensitive markets. This may be a premium-only trend that fails to scale. Internal POS has no data to validate. Competitor ads and YouTube buzz are unknown.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, full-price products, high ratings, clean signal

myntra.reason: Moderate presence, low discount, strategic pricing, clean signal

meesho.reason: Absent — no products, no resellers, zero demand signal

eval-premium-mirage.bet_rationale: Nykaa and Myntra show clean premium demand, but Meesho absence and lack of internal data create uncertainty. A small trial in premium channels can validate real demand before wider commitment.
eval-premium-mirage.key_uncertainty: Will the trend gain traction in value-fashion channels (Meesho) or remain premium-only?

eval-mass-only.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Meesho shows strong volume but Nykaa absent and Myntra discount-dependent; no internal or social data to confirm.

conflicts.proves: Demand exists at ₹199 but not at ₹800+.

conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate presence at 35% discount; unclear driver.

convergences.title: Price-sensitive demand confirmed
convergences.detail: Both Myntra (discounted) and Meesho (low price) show demand at value price points.
convergences.cannot_prove: Willingness to pay full price or premium.

eval-mass-only.upside_summary: Meesho shows strong volume with accelerating reseller growth, indicating genuine demand in price-sensitive markets. The style has traction at ₹199, suggesting potential for volume-driven success.
eval-mass-only.catch_summary: Nykaa absence and Myntra's discount dependency raise concerns about premium appeal. No internal, ad, or social data to validate trend strength or sustainability. Demand may be purely price-driven.

eval-golden-convergence.headline: All sources converge: Golden Kurti shows clean demand across premium, mass, and value segments with no discount distortion

convergences.title: Cross-segment demand convergence
convergences.detail: Nykaa (premium, full-price), Myntra (mass, low discount), and Meesho (value, high volume) all show strong positive signals without discount distortion.
convergences.proves: The style has broad appeal across price tiers; demand is genuine and not discount-driven.
convergences.cannot_prove: Long-term sustainability or whether internal POS will replicate; no competitor ad or social data to confirm organic trend.

eval-golden-convergence.upside_summary: Golden Kurti shows strong, clean demand across premium (Nykaa), mass (Myntra), and value (Meesho) segments. No discount distortion detected. Reseller growth on Meesho indicates expanding reach.
eval-golden-convergence.catch_summary: No internal POS data to validate against your store performance. Absence of Meta Ads and YouTube data means we cannot assess whether demand is organic or paid-driven. Trend may be established but lacks social buzz confirmation.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence with full-price products and high ratings; clean signal.

meesho.reason: High volume and reseller growth; accelerating demand.

eval-golden-convergence.bet_rationale: All three external sources converge with clean signals and no discount distortion. The trend has broad appeal across price segments, warranting a wider rollout across stores.
eval-golden-convergence.key_uncertainty: Will internal POS confirm the same demand pattern without prior history?

eval-silent-meesho.headline: ⚡ Sources disagree: Premium demand on Nykaa but zero presence on Meesho and no social/ad validation

conflicts.title: Nykaa strong vs Meesho absent
conflicts.nykaa_says: Strong premium demand at full price (2 products, avg ₹2200)
conflicts.myntra_says: Moderate demand at slight discount (1 product, 20% off)
conflicts.meesho_says: No demand at all (0 products, 0 resellers)
conflicts.buyer_question: Is this a premium-only trend that won't work for value-fashion customers?
conflicts.proves: Premium segment shows interest; mass market shows none
conflicts.cannot_prove: Whether mass market will adopt if priced lower

convergences.title: Nykaa and Myntra both show positive demand
convergences.detail: Nykaa has 2 full-price products with high ratings; Myntra has 1 product with Strategic Value Pricing and good reviews
convergences.proves: There is some demand for this style in organized retail
convergences.cannot_prove: Whether demand is broad or niche

eval-silent-meesho.upside_summary: Nykaa shows strong premium demand at full price with high ratings, indicating genuine style preference. Myntra confirms moderate demand with clean discount context. If this trend expands to mass market, there could be significant upside.
eval-silent-meesho.catch_summary: Meesho absence is a major red flag for mass-market viability. No social buzz or competitor ad data to validate organic demand. The trend may be limited to premium buyers only.

nykaa.reason: Strong presence, full-price products, high ratings, clean signal

myntra.reason: Moderate presence, Strategic Value Pricing, good reviews, no noise flags

meesho.reason: Absent presence, no products or resellers

eval-silent-meesho.bet_rationale: Premium demand exists but mass-market absence and lack of social/ad validation create uncertainty. A small trial in premium stores can test real demand without overcommitting.
